You take a look in the shower. It has a shower curtain to hide the occupant. Inside are two areas: a changing area, and a shower area. Behind the curtain, you find .

The shower head is detachable from the wall and connected via a long cord so that you can spray your body from any angle if so desired. <desc><s>Several large sections of the room are sealed off from one another, each housing an aquaponics system.</s> <s>In these, plants grow above tanks of water which house a variety of fish, and each system is kept at a different temperature to accommodate the different plants and fish housed there.</s> <s>The separate systems are labeled SYSTEM 1, SYSTEM 2, SYSTEM 3, and SYSTEM 4.</s></desc>

<desc><s>This system is kept relatively warm; its sensors display the air temperature as 27.1°C and the water temperature as 26.0°C with a pH level of 6.1.</s> <s>Other displays show levels of nitrogen, magnesium, and other minerals, as well as optimal dates and times for cultivation.</s> <s>Along the walls of the system are TOMATO PLANTS, while LETTUCE GROW BEDS sit atop the tanks themselves.</s> <s>CHICKPEA PLANTS take up the excess space.</s> <s>Within the large tanks, beneath the plants, there are <il>BASS and WALLEYE</il> swimming lazily around.</s></desc>

<desc><s>These grow beds are essentially lattices of white plastic with each little compartment housing a separate head of lettuce.</s> <s>Their roots stretch down into the water below.</s> <s>Not all of them are ready to be harvested yet.</s> <s>Taking stock of what's fully mature here, you count <il></il>.</s></desc>
<desc><s>There are several rows of tomato plants growing along the edge of the tank.</s> <s>While some of the tomatoes are green, you see <il></il> ready for harvesting.</s></desc>
<desc><s>These leafy plants produce chickpeas, a legume with many uses.</s></desc>

These greenish fish bob up toward the surface of the water frequently, mingling with the dangling roots of the lettuce plants.

> Tip: You can `.take` one!

These walleye are fairly large fish with faint stripes. They shine yellow-gold, though it's hard to tell through this murky water.

> Tip: You can `.take` one!

<desc><s>This system isn't too warm or cold; its sensors display the air temperature as 20.7°C and the water temperature as 19.9°C with a pH level of 6.1.</s> <s>Other displays show levels of nitrogen, magnesium, and other minerals, as well as optimal dates and times for cultivation.</s> <s>Along the walls of the system are PEPPER PLANTS and CABBAGE BEDS.</s> <s>Floating on setups attached to the tanks themselves are CHIVE GROW RAFTS and ONION GROW RAFTS.</s> <s>There are two big tanks, containing <il>SALMON and PERCH</il>.</s></desc>

<desc><s>The cabbage grows in a white lattice with one head per box.</s> <s>It's attached to the side of the tank and floats on the surface of the water.</s> <s>You find <il></il> ready for harvesting.</s></desc>

<desc><s>These plants are each held in cylindrical tubing with gravel at the bottom.</s> <s>Their roots stretch into tanks through the seive-like bottoms of each cylinder.</s> <s>Among the plants, you find <il></il> ready for harvesting.</s></desc>

<desc><s>These grow rafts are lattices of white plastic with each little compartment housing a separate chive bulb.</s> <s>Not all of them are ready to be harvested yet.</s> <s>Taking stock of what's fully mature here, you count <il></il>.</s></desc>

<desc><s>These grow rafts are lattices of square boxes, and within each is an onion bulb.</s> <s>While some are still immature and growing, others are full-grown.</s> <s>Counting them, you find <il></il> ready to be harvested.</s></desc>

There are some perch swimming beneath the plants of this system. They're yellow with dark stripes, and they are somewhat smaller than the SALMON, which are segregated in a different tank.

> Tip: You can `.take` one!

There are a fair number of adult salmon swimming in the murky tank of this aquaponics system. The fish are long and silver in color.

> Tip: You can `.take` one!

<desc><s>This system is the warmest; its sensors display the air temperature as 27.7°C and the water temperature as 26.9°C with a pH level of 6.6.</s> <s>Other displays show levels of nitrogen, magnesium, and other minerals, as well as optimal dates and times for cultivation.</s> <s>This system is set up a bit differently than the others.</s> <s>It consists of a large tank housing <il>EGGPLANT GROW BEDS, CAULIFLOWER GROW BEDS, BASIL PLANTS, TILAPIA, and CATFISH</il>, which is connected to filtration system and tubes angled at a slight decline which connect the main tank to a smaller SUMP TANK.</s> <s>STRAWBERRY PLANTS grow in the tubes, and there are SHRIMP in the sump tank.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's a smaller sump tank attached to SYSTEM 3.</s> <s>It contains <il>SHRIMP</il>.</s></desc>
<desc><s>These long, quick-spreading plants have taken over the tubes connecting the large tank to the sump.</s> <s>You find <il></il> ready to be harvested among them.</s></desc>

<desc><s>The eggplants grow in a white plastic lattice with one plant per box.</s> <s>It's attached to the side of the tank and floats on the surface of the water.</s> <s>You find <il></il> ready for harvesting.</s></desc>
<desc><s>Basil plants line one edge of the large tank, taking up relatively little space.</s> <s>You find <il></il> ready for harvesting.</s></desc>

<desc><s>The cauliflower is set up similarly to the eggplants.</s> <s>It grows in a white plastic lattice with one plant per box.</s> <s>It's attached to the side of the tank and floats on the surface of the water.</s> <s>You find <il></il> ready for harvesting.</s></desc>

These fish are silver and more squarish in proportions than some of the other specimens here. They swim around contentedly in the tank below the grow beds.

> Tip: You can `.take` one!

You take a look at the catfish. These bottom-feeders are... well... feeding at the bottom of the tank, seemingly unbothered by their tilapia roommates.

> Tip: You can `.take` one!

Inside the sump tank, there are shrimp feeding away at the waste inside. They are grayish in color and cling to the tank with their tiny feet, feeling around with their antennae.

> Tip: You can `.take` one!

<desc><s>This system is the coldest of the four; its sensors display the air temperature as 14.7°C and the water temperature as 14.1°C with a pH level of 6.7.</s> <s>Other displays show levels of nitrogen, magnesium, and other minerals, as well as optimal dates and times for cultivation.</s> <s>Much of the system is taken up by SOY GROW BEDS, with CUCUMBER PLANTS taking up the remaining surface area.</s> <s>Within the tank, you only find <il>TROUT</il>.</s></desc>
<desc><s>These large grow beds contain stretches of soy plants that take up 2/3rds of the tank's surface area.</s> <s>Dozens of pods of SOYBEANS are ripe for the harvesting here.</s></desc>

<desc><s>These cucumber plants are each held in cylindrical tubing with gravel at the bottom.</s> <s>Their roots reach into the tank through the seive-like bottoms of each cylinder.</s> <s>Among the plants, you find <il></il> ready for harvesting.</s></desc>

There are dozens of trout swimming around in this tank, each with large, unblinking eyes.

> Tip: You can `.take` one!

<desc><s>It's an analog clock.</s> <if cond="this.childPuzzle.solved === true"><s>The time is currently showing as <var v="findPuzzle('HOUR DIAL').outcome" />:<if cond="parseInt(findPuzzle('MINUTE DIAL').outcome) < 10">0</if><var v="findPuzzle('MINUTE DIAL').outcome" />, although the clock doesn't seem to be functioning.</s></if><if cond="this.childPuzzle.solved === false"><s>The hour is currently showing as <var v="findPuzzle('HOUR DIAL').outcome" />, but there doesn't seem to be a minute hand, and the clock doesn't seem to be functioning in the first place.</s></if> <s>The clock has a GLASS COVER to protect its face, which is currently <if cond="findPuzzle('GLASS COVER').solved === true">open</if><if cond="findPuzzle('GLASS COVER').solved === false">closed and locked</if>.</s> <s>Just beneath the clock are an HOUR DIAL and a MINUTE DIAL which you can use to move the clock hands.</s></desc>

<desc><s>It's a small dial to move the hour hand on the clock.</s> <s>By rotating it, you can move the dial from hour 1 - 12.</s> <s>(Note: To set the hour dial, send `.use hour dial #`, replacing the # with the number you want to select.)</s></desc>

<desc><s>It's a small dial to move the minute hand on the clock.</s> <s>By rotating it, you can move the dial from minute 0 - 59.</s> <if cond="findPuzzle('CLOCK', 'rec-room').solved === false"><s>Or at least you *could*, if there was a minute hand on the clock.</s></if><if cond="findPuzzle('CLOCK', 'rec-room').solved === true"><s>(Note: To set the minute dial, send `.use minute dial #`, replacing the # with the number you want to select.)</s></if></desc>

<desc><s>It looks fairly old with a wood casing.</s> <if cond="findPuzzle('TIME', 'rec-room').solved === true"><s>It has a digital touch screen allowing you to select from several music tracks to play: TOXIC, NEVER GONNA GIVE YOU UP, OLD TOWN ROAD, GANGNAM STYLE, GLOOMY SUNDAY, CALL ME MAYBE, PARTY ROCK ANTHEM, and GANGSTERS PARADISE.</s> <if cond="findEvent('TOXIC').ongoing === true"><s>"Toxic" by Britney Spears is currently playing.</s></if><if cond="findEvent('NEVER GONNA GIVE YOU UP').ongoing === true"><s>"Never Gonna Give You Up" by Rick Astley is currently playing.</s></if><if cond="findEvent('OLD TOWN ROAD').ongoing === true"><s>"Old Town Road" by Lil Nas X is currently playing.</s></if><if cond="findEvent('GANGNAM STYLE').ongoing === true"><s>"Gangnam Style" by Psy is currently playing.</s></if><if cond="findEvent('CALL ME MAYBE').ongoing === true"><s>"Call Me Maybe" by Carly Rae Jepsen is currently playing.</s></if><if cond="findEvent('PARTY ROCK ANTHEM').ongoing === true"><s>"Party Rock Anthem" by LMFAO is currently playing.</s></if><if cond="findEvent('GANGSTERS PARADISE').ongoing === true">"Gangster's Paradise" by Coolio is currently playing.</s></if><if cond="findEvent('GLOOMY SUNDAY').ongoing === true"><s>An announcement from a digitized voice is currently playing.</s> <s>"-In the infirmary that requires certain credentials to access. The username is `Cella`. The password is `Acuity`."</s></if> <s>(Note: In order to play a song, send `.use jukebox TITLE`. The title must be given in all caps.)</s></if><if cond="findPuzzle('TIME', 'rec-room').solved === false"><s>It doesn't seem to be powered on, and you can't find any way to do so.</s></if></desc>

<desc><s>It's about the size of a standard air hockey table you would find at an actual arcade.</s> <s>The table has <il></il> placed on it.</s> <s>Pushing the red button turns the air on or off, allowing you to play.</s></desc>

It's an electronic dartboard hung on the wall. It consists of a circle with 20 numbered stokes, with two rings and a bullseye. There's seemingly no pattern to the point values assigned to each stoke, but they're numbered from 1 - 20. If you land a dart in the outer ring, you earn double the points of whatever stoke that section of the outer ring belongs to, and the inner ring results in triple the points. A bullseye will net you 50 points.

There's currently a dart lodged in the board resulting in points and another dart lodged in the board resulting in another points.

Beneath the board itself is a small COMPARTMENT.

(Note: To enter the desired point values, make sure you have a dart in your inventory before each shot, and send `.use first dart #`, then `.use second dart #`, then `.use third dart #`. Note that some point values may not be possible.)

It's a small compartment below the dartboard. It's currently open. Written on it is "Prime x Prime x Prime = 266". There doesn't seem to be any way to open it.

Maybe it will open if you hit three prime numbers on the dartboard that multiply together to make 266. If that's the case, then you know a prime number is a number whose only products are 1 and itself. You don't even have to try any of the double or triple point values, or 50 for that matter. The only prime numbers on this board would be 2, 3, 5, 7, 11, 13, 17, and 19. Better yet, 266 is an even number, so you know one of the products MUST be 2, and you only need to find the other two numbers. This should be easy.

(Note: Due to the bot's limitations, you should enter the numbers in increasing order, or else you cannot solve the puzzle.)

<desc><s>The shelves take up huge portions of the walls.</s> <s>Written on each one is "Empire Pharmaceuticals CAS Food Storage Shelves™".</s> <s>There are three of them: SHELF 1, SHELF 2, and SHELF 3.</s></desc>

<desc><s>The shelf on the left wall consists of what must be hundreds of small drawers, each one labeled and with a button that you have to press to open the drawer.</s> <s>In them, you find <il>BREAD ROLLS, TOAST, GARLIC BREAD, BAGELS, BROCCOLI, CARROTS, GREEN BEANS, PEAS, BEANS, FRENCH FRIES, MASHED POTATOES, KIMCHI, PAD THAI, RAMEN, RICE, RICE AND MUSHROOMS, TAKOYAKI, TTEOKBOKKI, YAKISOBA, and MACARONI AND CHEESE</il>.</s></desc>

<desc><s>The left shelf on the back wall consists of what must be hundreds of small drawers, each one labeled and with a button that you have to press to open the drawer.</s> <s>In them, you find <il>CHICKEN BREASTS, CHICKEN NUGGETS, TERIYAKI CHICKEN, TURKEYS, STEAKS, HAMBURGERS, CHEESEBURGERS, TACOS, BURRITOS, ROAST BEEF, PORK CHOPS, BACON, BARBECUE RIBS, HAMS, HOT DOGS, CORN DOGS, FISH STICKS, SPAGHETTI AND MEATBALLS, CHILI, and LASAGNA</il>.</s></desc>

<desc><s>The right shelf on the back wall consists of what must be hundreds of small drawers, each one labeled and with a button that you have to press to open the drawer.</s> <s>In them, you find <il>CHICKEN NOODLE SOUP, CREAM OF MUSHROOM SOUP, ALPHABET SOUP, TOMATO SOUP, APPLE PIE, RASPBERRY PIE, CHOCOLATE CAKE, CHEESE CAKE, CHOCOLATE CHIP COOKIES, SUGAR COOKIES, BROWNIES, PANCAKES, WAFFLES, TWINKIES, POPCORN, CHEESE PIZZAS, PEPPERONI PIZZAS, SPAGHETTIOS, MOZZARELLA STICKS, and OMELETTES</il>.</s></desc>

<desc><s>It's a regular, rectangular table surrounded by CHAIRS.</s> <s>You suppose you could eat here if you wanted to, but it's a little chilly in here...</s> <s>You find <il></il> on it.</s></desc>
<desc><s>There are 8 chairs surrounding the table.</s> <s>They're pretty standard.</s></desc>

It's the patented Empire Electronics Smart Cooker! It's capable of cooking almost anything in one minute. It looks kind of like a microwave, as a red box with a door that opens from the side. How exactly it cooks is a closely guarded trade secret, but it's truly an amazing piece of technology. It's currently powered on, with `` remaining. Inside, you find .

> Tip: You can cook with Alter Ego! To do so, `.take` one of the frozen food items from the shelves, `.drop` it in the cooker, and then activate the cooker by sending `.use COOKER`.
<desc><s>It's a large trash can for throwing things away.</s> <s>If you wanted to, you could probably hide inside it, but that'd be super gross.</s> <s>You find <il></il> tossed inside it.</s></desc>

<desc><s>The floor is covered with shiny black and brown tiles.</s> <s>You find <il></il> placed haphazardly on it.</s></desc>

<desc><s>There are several tables spread throughout the room, each one with two or three chairs surrounding it.</s> <s>They're all made out of metal and covered with a white tablecloth.</s> <s>You find <il></il> on one of them.</s></desc>

<desc><s>The bar is a sleek black material with red barstools in front of it.</s> <s>Underneath it is a MINI FRIDGE, a SINK, a MIXING STATION, and a BAR SHELF.</s> <s>There are also a variety of nozzles, including a BEER NOZZLE, a COLA NOZZLE, a GINGER ALE NOZZLE, a TONIC NOZZLE, and a ROOT BEER NOZZLE.</s> <s>You also find <il></il> left on the counter.</s> <s>Behind the bar counter is a large SHELF.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's a mini fridge with a built-in freezer compartment.</s> <s>You find <il></il>.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's a regular sink for all of the glasses and dishes the bar uses.</s> <s>In it, you find <il></il>.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's a space behind the bar counter for mixing drinks.</s> <s>You find <il></il> on it.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's a shelf to store all of the clean dishes that the bar uses.</s> <s>On it, you find <il></il>.</s></desc>

<desc><s>You take a look at the beer nozzle.</s> <s>It's connected by a hose to the bar.</s> <s>You can pick it up and literally shoot the liquid into a glass.</s> <s>It seems to dispense generic beer.</s> <s>Under it, you find <il></il>.</s></desc>

<desc><s>You take a look at the cola nozzle.</s> <s>It's connected by a hose to the bar.</s> <s>You can pick it up and literally shoot the liquid into a glass.</s> <s>This is likely what they use to make a whiskey coke or a rum and coke.</s> <s>That said, it's not Coca Cola.</s> <s>It's just... generic cola?</s> <s>Under it, you find <il></il>.</s></desc>

<desc><s>You take a look at the ginger ale nozzle.</s> <s>It's connected by a hose to the bar.</s> <s>You can pick it up and literally shoot the liquid into a glass.</s> <s>It seems to dispense generic ginger ale.</s> <s>Under it, you find <il></il>.</s></desc>

<desc><s>You take a look at the tonic nozzle.</s> <s>It's connected by a hose to the bar.</s> <s>You can pick it up and literally shoot the liquid into a glass.</s> <s>This is probably used for drinks such as gin and tonic.</s> <s>Under it, you find <il></il>.</s></desc>

<desc><s>You take a look at the root beer nozzle.</s> <s>It's connected by a hose to the bar.</s> <s>You can pick it up and literally shoot the liquid into a glass.</s> <s>Under it, you find <il></il>.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's a shelf filled with a variety of alcohol bottles.</s> <s>On it, you find <il></il>.</s></desc>

<desc><s>It's a small stage to hold musical performances.</s> <s>There's a PIANO on it, as well as a KARAOKE MACHINE.</s> <s>You also find <il></il> placed on it.</s> <s>A CURTAIN covers the back wall.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's a shiny, black, grand piano.</s> <s>It has an adjustable seat.</s> <s>Maybe you could play a song or two on it.</s></desc>

<desc><s>It's a modern karaoke machine.</s> <s>There's a touch screen attached, where you can type in the name of any song you want, and it'll play the instrumental version for you so you can sing to the lyrics on screen.</s> <s>It has a built-in microphone.</s></desc>

<desc><s>It's a red velvet curtain covering the back wall.</s><if cond="player.perception > 5"> <s>There doesn't seem to be any reason for it, as there's nothing behind it but a wall.</s> <s>You suppose it must just be here for decoration.</s></if></desc>
<desc><s>It's a medium-sized trash can for throwing things away.</s> <s>You find <il></il> in it.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's a black, plastic dome on a white, circular shell attached to the ceiling.</s> <s>It gives you the feeling that you're being watched.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's a small, flat-screen television with a beige, plastic border.</s></desc>

<desc><s>The floor in this room is covered in brown tiles, and it looks like it's been mopped recently.</s> <s>You find <il></il> placed haphazardly on it.</s> <s>There are several small, circular drains spread throughout the room.</s></desc>

It's really just four stainless steel tables put together, creating a large surface that's perfect for preparing dishes. You find a CUTTING BOARD and a TOFU MOLD on it. Beneath the station are RACKS for pots and pans.

> Tip: Want to combine multiple ingredients, but you can't `.craft` them because there's too many? Try putting them all here, and then `.use PREP STATION`! When in doubt, use the `.recipes` command.
<desc><s>This is a plain, stainless steel board used for food preparation.</s> <s>On it, you find <il></il>.</s></desc>

<desc><s>This is a series of square molds designed to shape soft tofu into firmer bits.</s> <s>There's muslin cloth available to line the molds and a large, heavy press you can lower down over the squares.</s> <if cond="this.activated === true"><s>The press is currently lowered down to make tofu.</s></if><if cond="this.activated !== true"><s>The press is upright, so you can see the squares inside.</s> <s>Here, you find <il></il>.</s></if></desc>
<desc><s>You check the racks underneath the PREP STATIONS.</s> <s>They contain <il></il>.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's a long, white countertop.</s> <s>It's broken up only by a SINK in the middle.</s> <s>On it, you find <il>a BLENDER and a MIXER</il>.</s></desc>

This is a large, stainless steel sink with a long, curved faucet head. It has two knobs, one for hot water and one for cold water, and it has a nozzle you can extend to spray things from different angles. The faucet is currently running. In it, you find a scrubbing brush.

> Tip: When some fixtures are turned on, they can transform the items they contain into different items, via recipes. Some fixtures (like this sink) will continually process items they contain, as long as they're turned on. So, if you wash something with dish detergent, and the item it turns into can be filled with water from the sink, it will automatically start trying to fill it with water once it's clean. If you don't want that to happen, be sure to turn it off as soon as you're done using it!

<desc><s>This looks like a run of the mill blender.</s> <s>You could use this to blend fruit or possibly other things.</s> <if cond="this.activated === true"><s>It's currently powered on.</s></if> <s>In it is <il></il>.</s></desc>

<desc><s>This is an electric mixer used to easily mix together semi-solids like dough and batter.</s> <if cond="this.activated === true"><s>It's currenty powered on.</s> </if><s>Inside, you find <il></il>.</s></desc>
<desc><s>You look through the cabinets over the counters.</s> <s>They're quite spacious.</s> <s>Inside, you find <il></il>.</s></desc>
<desc><s>You open the cupboard and look inside.</s> <s>It's very spacious.</s> <s>Here, you find <il></il>.</s></desc>

<desc><s>The refrigerator is made of stainless steel and opens up with two doors.</s> <s>There are two primariy shelves in it: SHELF 1 and SHELF 2.</s> <s>At the bottom are two crispers: CRISPER 1 and CRISPER 2.</s> <s>There doesn't seem to be a freezer.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's the top shelf of the refrigerator.</s> <s>This is where completed dishes go if they're not immediately eaten.</s> <s>On it, you find <il></il>.</s></desc>

<desc><s>It's the bottom shelf of the refrigerator.</s> <s>It has a lot more vertical space, and seems to be used to store ingredients that haven't been cooked yet.</s> <s>On it, you find <il></il>.</s></desc>

<desc><s>You pull out the left crisper.</s> <s>It seems to mostly contain fruits and vegetables.</s> <s>It has been partitioned into smaller sections to separate different foods.</s> <s>In the crisper, you find <il></il>.</s></desc>

<desc><s>You pull out the right crisper.</s> <s>It seems to mostly contain leafy greens and other vegetables.</s> <s>It has been partitioned into smaller sections to separate different foods.</s> <s>In the crisper, you find <il></il>.</s></desc>

It's a silver, pristine oven that shows light signs of wear. There is a knob along the top of it that allow you to set the temperature. It's currently powered on. You find in it.

On top of the oven are four burners: BURNER 1, BURNER 2, BURNER 3, and BURNER 4.
<desc><s>It's a high-powered electric stovetop burner.</s><if cond="this.activated"> <s>It's currently powered on.</s></if> <s>On it, you find <il></il>.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's a high-powered electric stovetop burner.</s><if cond="this.activated"> <s>It's currently powered on.</s></if> <s>On it, you find <il></il>.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's a high-powered electric stovetop burner.</s><if cond="this.activated"> <s>It's currently powered on.</s></if> <s>On it, you find <il></il>.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's a high-powered electric stovetop burner.</s><if cond="this.activated"> <s>It's currently powered on.</s></if> <s>On it, you find <il></il>.</s></desc>

This is a large, clear storage drum filled with dry soybeans. It's connected to a CONTAINER near its base by a hard plastic tube with a lever attached. You can `.use` it to fill the CONTAINER with soybeans.

> Tip: In this game, soybeans are a staple ingredient that can be transformed into many different products that you can use for cooking. Before you turn on the CONTAINER, try filling it with multiple dry soybeans first.

This is a metallic container designed to rapidly prepare dried soybeans for preparation in cooking. It's connected to the VAT of soybeans by a tube on one side. Its lid is sealed shut and it's emitting a soft humming noise.Its lid is open. Inside, you find .

> Tip: Confused about what to do with these WET SOYBEANS? Try taking one, and then sending `.recipes WET SOYBEANS`. Specifying an item with the `.recipes` command will tell you all recipes that you can do with it, not just ones that are currently available to you.

<desc><s>There are a few different CLEANER DISPENSERS installed in the wall, with hoses connected.</s> <s>The mop sink also has a SPRAYER dangling down from a pipe in the ceiling.</s> <s>Beside the mop sink is a BROOM RACK.</s></desc>
<desc><s>These dispensers dispense three kinds of water: sanitizer water to wipe down surfaces, mop water for mopping the floors, and a general all-purpose cleaner for any other needs.</s></desc>

<desc><s>It's stainless steel and dangles down from the coiled rubber piping that is hanging from a metal pipe in the ceiling.</s> <s>When you hold down the trigger, it sprays high-pressure water at anything you aim it at.</s> <s>This is meant for cleaning.</s> <s>Nothing else.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's a rack on the wall for hanging brooms.</s> <s>Currently, there are <il></il> hanging on it.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's a large trash can for throwing things away.</s> <s>If you wanted to, you could probably hide inside it, but that'd be super gross.</s> <s>You find <il></il> tossed inside it.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's a black, plastic dome on a white, circular shell attached to the ceiling.</s> <s>It gives you the feeling that you're being watched.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's a small, flat-screen television with a beige, plastic border.</s></desc>
<desc><s>The floor is made of teal tiles, and it's so polished that it shines under the fluorescent lighting of the room.</s> <s>You find <il></il> on it.</s></desc>
<desc><s>There are three hospital beds, each one separated by CURTAINS: BED 1, BED 2, and BED 3.</s></desc>

<desc><s>It's an average hospital bed with a white mattress and blue sheets.</s> <s>On it, you find <il></il>.</s> <s>Near the bottom of the sheet is a drawing of a circle with a short straight line pointing directly to the left from the center.</s> <if cond="player.perception > 3"><s>It resembles the hour hand of a clock pointing where the 9 would be.</s></if></desc>

<desc><s>It's an average hospital bed with a white mattress and blue sheets.</s> <s>On it, you find <il></il>.</s> <s>Near the bottom of the sheet is a drawing of a circle with a long straight line pointing directly down from the center.</s> <if cond="player.perception > 4"><s>It resembles the minute hand of a clock pointing where the 6 would be.</s></if> <if cond="player.perception > 5"><s>So, 30 minutes past the hour.</s></if></desc>
<desc><s>It's an average hospital bed with a white mattress and blue sheets.</s> <s>On it, you find <il></il>.</s></desc>
<desc><s>There are three sets of curtains, which you can pull around each bed to give the occupant a bit of privacy.</s></desc>

<desc><s>It's a wall-mounted box for an AED, or Automated External Defibrillator<if cond="player.perception > 4">, a medical device that can monitor a person's heart rate and, if necessary, deliver an electric shock to initiate a regular heartbeat</if>.</s> <s>Inside is <il></il>.</s></desc>

<desc><s>It's an operating table for a surgeon to operate on a patient.</s> <s>You can rotate it 360 degrees, extend it for taller patients, and adjust its height.</s> <s>It's extremely well-lit thanks to the high-power surgical lights above it.</s> <s>On it, you find <il></il>.</s></desc>

<desc><s>It's a large, white tube that resembles the luggage compartment on an airplane.</s> <s>It's suspended from the ceiling and has several compartments in which to store surgical equipment.</s> <s>In it, you find <il>various miscellaneous surgical instruments</il>.</s></desc>

<desc><s>It's a granite countertop, which is exceptionally clean.</s> <s>There's a SINK embedded in it as well as a MIRROR and air dryer on the wall above it.</s> <s>You find <il></il> on the countertop.</s> <s>Beneath the counter is a TRASH CAN.</s></desc>

<desc><s>It's a metal sink.</s> <s>It has two knobs, one for hot water and one for cold water, and the water pressure is nice.</s> <s>It looks impeccably clean.</s> <if cond="this.activated === true"><s>The faucet is currently running.</s></if> <s>In it, you find <il></il>.</s></desc>

It's a mirror hung on the wall above the counter. You can see your reflection in it:
>>>
<desc><s>It's a plastic bin for throwing things away.</s> <s>You find <il></il> in it.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's a set of metallic cupboards above the COUNTER.</s> <s>Inside, you find <il></il>.</s></desc>

<desc><s>It's a large cabinet for medical supplies.</s> <s>It has a COMBINATION LOCK attached to it.</s> <if cond="this.childPuzzle.solved === true"><s>It's unlocked.</s> <var v="this.childPuzzle.alreadySolvedDescription.parseFor(player)" /></if><if cond="this.childPuzzle.solved === false"><s>It's currently locked.</s></if></desc>

<desc><s>The combination consists of 4 sets of letters, with 3 letters in each set.</s> <s>There's a little scribble above it that says "+1".</s> <if cond="player.perception > 5"><s>Maybe that means that whatever clue you find to the combination, you should change each letter to the next letter in the alphabet.</s></if> <if cond="player.perception > 7"><s>If one of the letters in the clue happened to be Z, you'd probably just loop back around and change it to A.</s></if> <s>(Note: The format for the combination is AAA AAA AAA AAA, and all letters must be uppercase.)</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's a large cabinet for medicine.</s> <s>It has several shelves containing a variety of medicines.</s> <s>Among them, you find <il></il>.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's a pair of metal cabinets, one on top of the other.</s> <s>On the bottom is a large SUPPLY CABINET.</s> <s>On top is a slightly smaller MEDICINE CABINET.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's a metallic desk with a DRAWER built into it.</s> <s>Much of the desks's surface is taken up by a COMPUTER.</s> <s>However, you also find <il></il> on it.</s></desc>

<desc><s>It's a fairly modern looking computer, set up with a keyboard and mouse -- just the bare necessities.</s> <s>It doesn't seem to have any inputs.</s> <if cond="this.childPuzzle.solved === true"><var v="this.childPuzzle.alreadySolvedDescription.parseFor(player)" /></if><if cond="this.childPuzzle.solved === false"><s>The computer is asking for a USERNAME and PASSWORD.</s></if>  <s>(Note: To log in, send `.use USERNAME ` followed by the username, then `.use PASSWORD ` followed by the password, then `.use LOGIN`.)</s></desc>

You click on the icon for the upload program. It shows you a simple dialog box with a prompt:

> Upload facility map to tablets?
> `UPLOAD`
<desc><s>It's a button at the bottom lower left of the screen.</s> <s>You can log out of the current account with this.</s></desc>
<desc><s>There are two posters: POSTER 1 and POSTER 2.</s></desc>

<desc><s>This is the leftmost poster.</s> <s>It depicts a human that's translucent in such a way that you can see all of their organs.</s> <s>Each organ is numbered with a list in the top right corner naming each organ shown.</s> <s>There are also several diagrams showing close ups of several organs with lines pointing to various structures within each organ.</s></desc>

<desc><s>This is the rightmost poster.</s> <s>It seems to be an eye chart to test a patient's vision.</s> <s>There's a line of text on the bottom that's so small you need a magnifying glass to read it.</s> <if cond="findInventoryItem('MAGNIFYING GLASS', player.name) !== undefined"><s>You use your MAGNIFYING GLASS to read the text, which is as follows: "HBD KKZ RDD ZKK".</s></if></desc>

<desc><s>It's a digital scale that displays the current weight in kilograms.</s> <s>On it is <il></il>.</s> <if cond="findFlag('INFIRMARY SCALE WEIGHT', true) === 0"><s>It's powered off right now since there's nothing on it.</s> <s>Pressing the button just to make sure, it reads `0.00 kg`.</s> <s>At least you know it's functional.</s></if><if cond="findFlag('INFIRMARY SCALE WEIGHT') !== 0"><s>Right now, the scale reads `<var v="findFlag('INFIRMARY SCALE WEIGHT').toFixed(2)" /> kg`.</s> <s>In pounds, that's `<var v="findFlag('INFIRMARY SCALE WEIGHT') * 2.20" /> lb`.</s></if></desc>

<desc><s>It's a long ruler attached to the wall with an adjustable metal sheet jutting out that you can move to the top of someone's head.</s> <s>This allows you to measure a person's height by having them stand against the wall.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's a black, plastic dome on a white, circular shell attached to the ceiling.</s> <s>It gives you the feeling that you're being watched.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's a small, flat-screen television with a beige, plastic border.</s></desc>
<desc><s>The floor here is made of concrete.</s> <s>You find <il></il> placed haphazardly on it.</s></desc>

<desc><s>You look through the shelves.</s> <s>There are several rows of them, but they mostly contain cardboard boxes with nothing interesting inside them.</s> <s>Each shelf has a different category, though, and some of them stand out: a CHEMICAL SHELF, a TOOL SHELF, an ELECTRONICS SHELF, and a MISCELLANEOUS SHELF.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's the chemical shelf.</s> <s>On it, you find <il></il>.</s> <s>There are various other chemicals, but none of them seem to be of any interest.</s></desc>

<desc><s>It's the tool shelf.</s> <s>As you might expect, it contains tools.</s> <s>On it, you find <il></il>.</s> <s>There are several other random tools on the shelf, but none of them look particularly useful.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's the electronics shelf.</s> <s>It's filled with mostly old and broken electronics, but there are some things that might be useful.</s> <s>On it, you find <il></il>.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's the miscellaneous shelf.</s> <s>The items on it belong to no particular category.</s> <s>Some that draw your attention are <il></il>.</s></desc>
<desc><s>These racks have a variety of useful goods on them that are necessary for daily life.</s> <s>On them, you find <il></il>.</s></desc>

<desc><s>There seems to be a spare of each piece of furniture found in the dorms.</s> <s>There's a BED, a NIGHTSTAND, a DRESSER, and even a MIRROR leaning against the wall.</s> <s>Among it all, you also find <il></il>.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's a twin bed with white sheets.</s> <s>On it, you find <il></il>.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's a black, metal nightstand.</s> <s>Thankfully, its corners aren't too sharp.</s> <s>It has a TOP DRAWER and a BOTTOM DRAWER.</s> <s>On top of it is <il>a small lamp</il>.</s></desc>
<desc><s>You look inside the top drawer.</s> <s>In it, you find <il></il>.</s> <if cond="this.containsNoItems() === true"><s>It's empty.</s></if></desc>
<desc><s>You look inside the bottom drawer.</s> <s>In it, you find <il></il>.</s> <if cond="this.containsNoItems() === true"><s>It's empty.</s></if></desc>

<desc><s>It's a black, metal dresser, flat to the ground with no sign of budging.</s> <s>It's got three drawers, each with a white handle; a TOP DRESSER DRAWER, a MIDDLE DRESSER DRAWER, and a BOTTOM DRESSER DRAWER.</s> <s>On top of it you find <il></il>.</s></desc>
<desc><s>You open the top dresser drawer.</s> <s>Inside you see <il></il>.</s> <if cond="this.containsNoItems() === true"><s>There's nothing inside.</s></if></desc>
<desc><s>You open the middle dresser drawer.</s> <s>Inside you see <il></il>.</s> <if cond="this.containsNoItems() === true"><s>It's empty.</s></if></desc>
<desc><s>You open the bottom dresser drawer.</s> <s>Inside you see <il>a SAFE</il>.</s></desc>

<desc><s>It's a small, box-shaped safe with a COMBINATION LOCK.</s> <if cond="this.childPuzzle.solved === true"><s>It's unlocked.</s> <var v="this.childPuzzle.alreadySolvedDescription.parseFor(player)" /></if></desc>
<desc><s>The combination consists of six digits from 0 - 9.</s> <s>(Note: The format for the combination is ######)</s></desc>

It's a mirror leaning against the wall. You can see your reflection in it:
>>>
<desc><s>Each poster goes to the floor, and they're all very tall, taller than anyone here.</s> <s>There are three of them: POSTER 1, POSTER 2, and POSTER 3.</s></desc>

<desc><s>It's the leftmost poster.</s> <s>It appears to be a lifesize print of Nuan Yu, a famous Chinese basketball player for the NBA.</s> <s>There's a short biography about her on the side.</s> <s>Although she's not the first Chinese player to play for the NBA, she is the first woman to do so on a formerly men's team, beginning with her draft pick in 2039.</s> <s>Since then, she's served as an inspiration to young female athletes everywhere.</s> <s>She stands at an impressive 6'5" tall.</s> <s>With crossed arms and a determined expression, she exudes strength and confidence.</s> <s>Written above her in a bold font is her slogan: "Take the shot."</s> <s>For some reason, the K in "take" is written in red and the E in "the" is written in blue.</s></desc>

<desc><s>It's the middle poster.</s> <s>It appears to be a print of a scroll with Chinese characters on it.</s> <s>It says "竜頭蛇尾".</s> <s>The English meaning is written in small print at the bottom: "dragon's head, serpent's tail".</s> <s>For some reason, the R in "dragon's" is written in purple and the L in "tail" is written in yellow.</s> <if cond="player.hasBehaviorAttribute('reads Japanese')"><s>Although the literal meaning is written at the bottom, this is actually, a *yojijukugo*, a Japanese idiom consisting of four kanji.</s> <s>This one in particular means "anticlimax".</s> <s>What a strange poster.</s></if></desc>

<desc><s>It's the rightmost poster.</s> <s>It appears to be promotional art for the video game *Persona 8*.</s> <s>It features a unique cast of characters all dressed in police uniforms, including a German shepherd mascot character.</s> <s>The game's tagline says "Let's create justice".</s> <s>For some reason, the L in "Let's" is written in green and the I in "justice" is written in orange.</s></desc>

<desc><s>It's a large yellow trash bin with a black bag inside.</s> <s>If you wanted to, you could probably hide inside it, but that'd be super gross.</s> <s>You find <il></il> tossed inside it.</s></desc>

<desc><s>It's a shallow, metal indentation in the wall in the shape of a square.</s> <s>It looks like it was made for you to snap something into it.</s> <s>(Note: To snap something into it, send `.use item name slot`)</s></desc>

<desc><s>It's a panel embedded in the wall.</s> <s>It's square in shape and made of metal, with a keypad consisting of the 26 letters of the alphabet and a small digital screen.</s> <if cond="findPuzzle('RED KEYPAD', 'storage').solved === false"><s>All of the buttons on the keypad are lit up in red.</s> <s>(Note: To enter something into the keypad, send `.use red keypad INPUT`, replacing "INPUT" with what you wish to enter in all capital letters.)</s></if><if cond="findPuzzle('RED KEYPAD', 'storage').solved === true && findPuzzle('ORANGE KEYPAD', 'storage').solved === false"><s>"K" is written on the screen.</s> <s>All of the buttons on the keypad are lit up in orange.</s> <s>(Note: To enter something into the keypad, send `.use orange keypad INPUT`, replacing "INPUT" with what you wish to enter in all capital letters.)</s></if><if cond="findPuzzle('ORANGE KEYPAD', 'storage').solved === true && findPuzzle('YELLOW KEYPAD', 'storage').solved === false"><s>"KI" is written on the screen.</s> <s>All of the buttons on the keypad are lit up in yellow.</s> <s>(Note: To enter something into the keypad, send `.use yellow keypad INPUT`, replacing "INPUT" with what you wish to enter in all capital letters.)</s></if><if cond="findPuzzle('YELLOW KEYPAD', 'storage').solved === true && findPuzzle('GREEN KEYPAD', 'storage').solved === false"><s>"KIL" is written on the screen.</s> <s>All of the buttons on the keypad are lit up in green.</s> <s>(Note: To enter something into the keypad, send `.use green keypad INPUT`, replacing "INPUT" with what you wish to enter in all capital letters.)</s></if><if cond="findPuzzle('GREEN KEYPAD', 'storage').solved === true && findPuzzle('BLUE KEYPAD', 'storage').solved === false"><s>"KILL" is written on the screen.</s> <s>All of the buttons on the keypad are lit up in blue.</s> <s>(Note: To enter something into the keypad, send `.use blue keypad INPUT`, replacing "INPUT" with what you wish to enter in all capital letters.)</s></if><if cond="findPuzzle('BLUE KEYPAD', 'storage').solved === true && findPuzzle('PURPLE KEYPAD', 'storage').solved === false"><s>"KILLE" is written on the screen.</s> <s>All of the buttons on the keypad are lit up in purple.</s> <s>(Note: To enter something into the keypad, send `.use purple keypad INPUT`, replacing "INPUT" with what you wish to enter in all capital letters.)</s></if><if cond="findPuzzle('PURPLE KEYPAD', 'storage').solved === true"><s>None of the buttons are lit up, and the screen is blank.</s> <s>The panel doesn't seem to do anything anymore.</s></if></desc>
<desc><s>It's a black, plastic dome on a white, circular shell attached to the ceiling.</s> <s>It gives you the feeling that you're being watched.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's a small, flat-screen television with a beige, plastic border.</s></desc>
<desc><s>The floor of this room is covered in a gray carpet with the occasional red target pattern for some reason.</s> <s>You find <il></il> placed haphazardly on it.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's just a plain, blue yoga mat.</s> <s>You find <il></il> on it.</s></desc>

<desc><s>It's fully functional, so you could use it to do some running if you wanted to, which would improve your speed and stamina.</s> <s>Strangely, it's the only device in this room powered by electricity, and it's plugged into an OUTLET in the corner.</s> <s>On the treadmill's PANEL is a screen and some buttons to set the speed.</s> <if cond="findPuzzle('TREADMILL PANEL', 'exercise-room').solved === true"><s>It's currently on, with the speed set to <var v="findPuzzle('TREADMILL PANEL', 'exercise-room').outcome" /> meters per second.</s> <if cond="player.calculateMoveRate(true) < findFlag('EXERCISE ROOM TREADMILL SPEED', true)"><s>You don't think you can handle that speed.</s> <s>You should probably lower it first.</s></if><if cond="player.calculateMoveRate(true) >= findFlag('EXERCISE ROOM TREADMILL SPEED')"><s>You think you can handle that speed.</s></if></desc>

<desc><s>It's a panel attached to the TREADMILL.</s> <s>Apparently the brand is called "Avogadro".</s> <s>On it is a screen to display the current speed in meters per second, as well as some buttons to change it.</s> <s>The screen can only display two numbers with a decimal point always between them, so you guess the limit is 9.9 meters per second.</s> <if cond="player.perception > 7"><s>Perhaps something will happen if you set the speed to Avogadro's constant?</s> <if cond="player.perception > 8"><s>Of course, there's no way to do that precisely.</s> <s>The closest you could get would just be 6.0.</s></if></if> <if cond="this.childPuzzle.solved === true">The speed is currently set to <var v="this.childPuzzle.outcome" /> meters per second.</s></if><if cond="this.childPuzzle.solved === false"><s>It's currently off.</s></if> <s>(Note: To set the speed to 2.5 for example, write `.use panel 2.5`. To turn it off, just do `.use panel`.)</s></desc>

<desc><s>It's a plain electrical outlet built into the wall.</s> <if cond="this.childPuzzle.solved === false"><s>The TREADMILL is plugged into it, but there's a slot to plug something else in, too.</s></if><if cond="this.childPuzzle.solved === true"><s>The TREADMILL and BUNSEN BURNER are plugged into it.</s></if></desc>

<desc><s>It's an electric Bunsen burner.</s> <s>It has a long electrical plug attached to it, which is currently plugged into the OUTLET.</s> <s>With this, you can carry out chemistry experiments with a flame.</s> <if cond="this.activated === true"><s>It's currently lit, a small flame coming out of the top.</s></if> <s>You find <il></il> over it.</s></desc>

<desc><s>It's a fully functional elliptical machine.</s> <s>On this, you can walk, jog, or run with no impact.</s> <s>It has arm handles that move in turn with the steps, providing a great upper and lower body workout, which would improve your speed and stamina.</s> <s>It doesn't require any electricity.</s></desc>

<desc><s>It's a fully functional exercise bike.</s> <s>You ride it just like a regular bicycle, but since it's stationary, you won't actually go anywhere.</s> <s>This would improve your speed and stamina.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's a small rack for weights that's tucked neatly into the corner of the room.</s> <s><il></il> are all that's on it.</s></desc>

<desc><s>This is a classic bench press machine.</s> <s>In order to use it, you attach the desired weights to both sides of a dumbbell, then lie down face-up on the bench and lift the dumbbell up and down as much as you can.</s> <s>This would improve your strength.</s> <s>It's recommended you have someone to spot you.</s> <s>There is <il></il> on the rack.</s></desc>

<desc><s>This machine is used to train your lats, or back muscles.</s> <s>To use it, you set the desired weight, sit on the bench with the lap bar just over your thighs to keep your legs in place, and pull the bar down to your shoulders repeatedly.</s> <s>Every time you pull the bar down, it pulls the weights up.</s> <s>This would improve your strength.</s></desc>

<desc><s>This machine is used to train your biceps.</s> <s>To use it, set the desired weight, sit on the bench with your chest and upper arms against the rest, grab the handlebars, and bend your elbows, lifting the weights.</s> <s>This would improve your strength.</s></desc>

<desc><s>This machine has a moving seat as well as a foot rest for your feet to go, as well as a handle on a long wire coming from the machine.</s> <s>Bending your knees and straightening them out pushes the seat forward and backward.</s> <s>The farther back you go, the farther you have to pull the handle, mimicking the rowing of a boat.</s> <s>This would give you a great full body workout.</s> <s>This would improve your strength and stamina.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's a simple bench with a black cushion.</s> <s>This would be a good spot to take a rest if you're fatigued.</s> <s>You find <il></il> placed on it.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's a black, plastic dome on a white, circular shell attached to the ceiling.</s> <s>It gives you the feeling that you're being watched.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's a small, flat-screen television with a beige, plastic border.</s></desc>
<desc><s>The floor here is made of concrete.</s> <s>On it, you find <il></il>.</s></desc>

<desc><s>It's a granite countertop spanning half the length of the room, with the other half comprised of SHELVES.</s> <s>It rests atop a set of CUPBOARDS.</s> <s>There are CABINETS above it, as well.</s> <s>On the counter itself, you find <il>a RED CLAY BIN, a WHITE CLAY BIN, and a SCALE</il>.</s></desc>

<desc><s>It's an electronic scale used to measure mass.</s> <s>This should be useful for measuring clay and glazes, so you can use just the right amount.</s> <s>On it is <il></il>.</s> <if cond="findFlag('CERAMICS STUDIO SCALE WEIGHT', true) === 0"><s>It's powered off right now since there's nothing on it.</s> <s>Pressing the button just to make sure, it reads `0.0 kg`.</s> <s>At least you know it's functional.</s></if><if cond="findFlag('CERAMICS STUDIO SCALE WEIGHT') !== 0"><s>Right now, the scale reads `<var v="findFlag('CERAMICS STUDIO SCALE WEIGHT').toFixed(1)" /> kg`.</s> <s>In pounds, that's `<var v="findFlag('CERAMICS STUDIO SCALE WEIGHT') * 2.2" /> lb`.</s></if></desc>

<desc><s>It's a set of wooden cupboards beneath the counter.</s> <s>There are several of them, mostly housing various carving tools, and large items needed for creating pottery.</s> <s>Of particular note, you find <il></il>.</s></desc>

<desc><s>It's a small set of wooden cabinets above the counter.</s> <s>There are several of them, mostly containing brushes and other tools for glazing sculptures.</s> <s>Of particular note, you find <il>a BASE GLAZE BIN and a PATTERN GLAZE BIN</il>.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's a plastic bin for storing bottles of base glaze.</s> <s>You'll need these if you want to glaze your sculpture.</s> <s>Inside, you find <il></il>.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's a plastic bin for storing bottles of pattern glaze.</s> <s>You'll need these if you want to paint a pattern on your sculpture.</s> <s>Inside, you find <il></il>.</s></desc>

<desc><s>It's a set of wooden shelves spanning half the length of the room, with the other half taken up by the COUNTER.</s> <s>It looks like this is where miscellaneous items are stored.</s> <s>On them, you find <il></il>.</s></desc>

It's a fairly large electric pottery wheel. It's comprised of a circular, flat disc called a wheel head, which spins as you press your foot on the pedal on the floor. The pottery wheel itself sits on a turquoise table-like machine with a flat surface to keep tools on. The machine has adjustable legs and an attached stool so you can keep your hands level with the clay. On top of the wheel head is . The power is off.

If you'd like to make something with clay, you'll have to place a lump of WET CLAY on a POTTERY BAT, then place the bat on pottery wheel.

Once it's in place, you can turn on the pottery wheel by sending `.use POTTERY WHEEL` followed by one of these options: `POT`, `PLATE`, `BOWL`, `MUG`, `TEAPOT`, `TEACUP`. It will take 30 seconds, during which you will be unable to move or stop this process.The pottery wheel is currently on, and being operated by .(Moderator's note: This process is shortened to 30 seconds for the purposes of the demo environment. If you would like to use this in a real game, it is recommended that this be changed to 30 minutes.)
<desc><s>It's a set of tables in the center of the room for working with clay.</s> <s>It looks like they're divided into MOLDING STATIONS and a GLAZING STATIONS.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's a table all set up for you to mold clay sculptures by hand. There are three stations: a ROLLING STATION, a FORMING STATION, and a CARVING STATION.</s></desc>

It's a station for one person to roll out their clay into a flat slab that's easy to work with. The station is all clear, giving you a nice, clean surface to work on. Now all you'll need is a lump of clay to flatten, and a rolling pin. On the surface of the rolling station, you find . Nobody is using it right now.

If you'd like to flatten a lump of clay so you can mold it into a sculpture, you'll need to place one here alongside a ROLLING PIN.

Then, you can set the thickness of your desired clay slab by sending `.use SLAB THICKNESS` followed by either `thick` or `thin`. The difference is only aesthetic.

Then, you can use the rolling station. It will take 5 seconds, during which you will be unable to move or stop this process.The rolling station is currently being used by .(Moderator's note: This process is shortened to 5 seconds for the purposes of the demo environment. If you would like to use this in a real game, it is recommended that this be changed to 5 minutes.)

It's a station for one person to cut, bend, form, score, and slip their clay into a sculpture. Laid out on its surface are clay cutting wires, tissue blades, rulers, sandpaper, and everything else you need to craft the perfect sculpture. Now you just need a slab of clay to work with. You also find . Nobody is using it right now.

If you'd like to form your clay into a sculpture, you'll need to roll out a lump of clay on the ROLLING STATION, and then put it here. Then, you need to set what item you want to make by sending `.use SCULPTURE FORM` followed by one of these options: `photo frame`, `cup`, `tray`, `dish`, `box`, `lantern`.

You'll also need to select which shape you want to use for the base by sending `.use SCULPTURE SHAPE` followed by one of these options: `square`, `rectangle`, `triangle`, `circle`, `oval`, `heart`, `diamond`, `star`.

Then, finally, you can use the forming station. It will take 30 minutes, during which you will be unable to move or stop this process.The forming station is currently being used by .

It's a station for one person to carve a pattern into their clay sculpture. The station is all clear. You find on its surface. Nobody is using it right now.

If you'd like to carve a pattern into your clay sculpture, you'll first need to form a slab of clay into a sculpture on the FORMING STATION, and then put it here along with an X ACTO KNIFE.

Then, you need to select what pattern you want to carve by sending `.use CARVING PATTERN` followed by one of these options: `vertical lines`, `horizontal lines`, `grid lines`, `columns`, `rows`, `chevrons`, `fans`, `square corners`, `holes`, `scales`, `flowers`, `hearts`.

You'll also need to select how deep you want to carve by sending `.use CARVING DEPTH` followed by one of these options: `convex`, `shallow`, `deep`, `hollow`.

Then, finally, you can use the carving station. It will take 30 seconds, during which you will be unable to move or stop this process.The carving station is currently being used by .(Moderator's note: This process is shortened to 30 seconds for the purposes of the demo environment. If you would like to use this in a real game, it is recommended that this be changed to 30 minutes.)

<desc><s>It's a table all set up for you to glaze your clay sculptures. You'll have to fire them in the kiln before you can glaze them here, though.</s> <s>There are two stations for you to work at: a BASE GLAZING STATION and a PATTERN GLAZING STATION.</s></desc>

It's a station for one person to glaze their clay sculpture. It seems this is where you apply the base glaze. Laid out on its surface are different-sized brushes and other tools you'll need. Now you just need a clay sculpture to work with, and a bottle of glaze. You also find . Nobody is using it right now.

If you'd like to glaze your sculpture, you'll need to put it here alongside a bottle of BASE GLAZE.

Then, you can use the base glazing station. It will take 30 seconds, during which you will be unable to move or stop this process.The base glazing station is currently being used by .(Moderator's note: This process is shortened to 30 seconds for the purposes of the demo environment. If you would like to use this in a real game, it is recommended that this be changed to 30 minutes.)

It's a station for one person to glaze their clay sculpture. It seems this is where you apply a pattern glaze. Laid out on its surface are different-sized brushes and other tools you'll need. Now you just need a clay sculpture to work with, and a bottle of glaze. You also find . Nobody is using it right now.

If you'd like to paint a pattern, you'll need to glaze your clay sculpture on the BASE GLAZING STATION, and then put it here alongside a bottle of PATTERN GLAZE.

Then, you can set the pattern you want to paint by sending `.use GLAZE PATTERN` followed by one of these options: `stripes`, `lines`, `rings`, `spots`, `stars`, `zigzagging lines`, `hearts`, `broken hearts`, `flames`, `webs`, `drip lines`, `flowers`, `trees`, `clouds`, `waves`, `bubbles`, `fish`, `turtles`, `sea creatures`, `rabbits`, `horses`, `farm animals`, `cats`, `dogs`, `birds`, `spiders`, `ants`, `beetles`.

Then, you can use the pattern glazing station. It will take 30 seconds, during which you will be unable to move or stop this process.The pattern glazing station is currently being used by .(Moderator's note: This process is shortened to 30 seconds for the purposes of the demo environment. If you would like to use this in a real game, it is recommended that this be changed to 30 minutes.)

<desc><s>It's a set of racks for drying your wet clay sculptures.</s> <s>You'll need to leave your sculpture here so it can dry out before you fire it in the kiln.</s> <s>There are four racks in all: DRYING RACK 1, DRYING RACK 2, DRYING RACK 3, and DRYING RACK 4.</s></desc>

It's a rack to dry your wet clay sculptures. You'll need to leave your sculpture here so it can dry out before you fire it in the kiln. There is on it. There's nothing on it right now, so you're clear to put a sculpture here.

-# (Note: You won't receive a notification when you put an item on the rack, or when it's finished drying.)

It's a rack to dry your wet clay sculptures. You'll need to leave your sculpture here so it can dry out before you fire it in the kiln. There is on it. There's nothing on it right now, so you're clear to put a sculpture here.

-# (Note: You won't receive a notification when you put an item on the rack, or when it's finished drying.)

It's a rack to dry your wet clay sculptures. You'll need to leave your sculpture here so it can dry out before you fire it in the kiln. There is on it. There's nothing on it right now, so you're clear to put a sculpture here.

-# (Note: You won't receive a notification when you put an item on the rack, or when it's finished drying.)

It's a rack to dry your wet clay sculptures. You'll need to leave your sculpture here so it can dry out before you fire it in the kiln. There is on it. There's nothing on it right now, so you're clear to put a sculpture here.

-# (Note: You won't receive a notification when you put an item on the rack, or when it's finished drying.)

It's a metal kiln, meant for firing clay. It's cylindrical in shape, almost like an oil drum, with four legs that plant it firmly on the ground. It's not particularly large; it might only be able to fit one sculpture inside. There's a red control panel on the front. It looks fairly straightforward to use.

It's currently on, firing at a very high temperature. The lid on top is locked in place, so you can't open it.It seems to be off right now. You open the lid to take a look inside. It looks like the interior is made of thin white bricks. Judging by the thickness and coloration, these are probably firebricks meant to provide insulation. The kiln currently contains . There's nothing inside right now, so the kiln is ready to use if you have something you'd like to fire.

It's a utility sink with a large, square, plastic basin on legs. Above it, there's a soap dispenser and paper towel dispenser, so you can wash your hands. The sink's basin is made of some kind of thick plastic, and the inside looks a bit disgusting. It contains . The faucet is running. Water flows out of it at a high pressure.The faucet isn't running right now.

Underneath the basin, the piping is visible. It looks like the drain leads to a SINK TRAP before water is allowed to leave the room through the pipes.

<desc><s>It's a white plastic container in the shape of a jar, connected to the pipes underneath the utility sink. It attaches to the pipes with screws. <if cond="player.perception > 5">Judging by its position, it looks like this collects clay particles, so that they don't flow into the rest of the plumbing system and clog it up.</if></s></desc>
<desc><s>This is a large, black trash can.</s> <s>Inside, you see <il>some dry clay scraps</il>.</s></desc>
<desc><s>The floor in this room is made up of EVA foam with a colorful puzzle piece pattern.</s> <s>You find <il></il> placed haphazardly on it.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's a large, black chalkboard built directly into the wall.</s> <s>Nothing is drawn on it.</s></desc>

<desc><s>It's a small purple rocking horse.</s> <s>It's made of some kind of thick plastic.</s> <s>You could try to ride this if you wanted to, but it probably won't be very comfortable, since it's made for kids.</s></desc>

<desc><s>It's rather small, only able to support the weight of a child, and the swing only sits a few inches above the ground when it's not in motion.</s> <s>It looks decently sturdy, but you probably shouldn't try it.</s></desc>

<desc><s>It's a seesaw, also known as a teeter totter.</s> <s>It's bright and colorful, made of some kind of strong metal.</s> <s>You could ride it if you wanted to, but it wasn't really made for adults.</s></desc>

It's a series of colorful play tunnels designed to look like the body of a cartoon caterpillar. These really aren't made for adults, but there could be something worthwhile inside. It might be a good place to hide.

(Note: If you would like to try going through the play tunnels, you should notify your moderator that this puzzle needs to be initialized.)You should be small enough to crawl inside of it. Send `.move PLAY TUNNELS` to go inside!There's no way you'll be able to fit inside. Do you know anyone who's small enough to go in there?

<desc><s>It's very low to the ground in order to accommodate children.</s> <s>It's all white and made of a thick plastic.</s> <s>It's surrounded by four CHAIRS.</s> <if cond="findPuzzle('CANDLE', 'playroom').solved === false || findEvent('CANDLE BURNING').ongoing === true"><s>You spot a CANDLE on the table.</s></if> <s>On the table, you find <il></il>.</s></desc>

<desc><s>It has a very simple black base, on top of which is a thin, white candlestick with a single wick.</s> <if cond="findEvent('CANDLE BURNING').ongoing === true"><s>It's currently lit, filling the room with a scent reminiscent of vanilla.</s></if></desc>
<desc><s>There are four chairs surrounding the table.</s> <s>They are all pink, and very short, made of a thick plastic.</s> <s>You find <il></il> sitting in one of them.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's a short, blue trampoline.</s> <s>It's made for children to bounce on.</s> <s>It would probably be a bad idea for you to try it.</s></desc>

<desc><s>It's rather big, at about 2 feet tall.</s> <s>It's primarily pink and white with an open face allowing you to move things around inside.</s> <s>It's very well decorated, with a small, happy family living their best lives.</s> <s>There's one room with a closed wall, but there's a plastic window looking inside it on the front.</s> <s>It's rather empty, but you do spot <il></il> lying on the floor in that room.</s> <s>There's a small opening on the side in a neighboring room.</s> <s>The opening is too small to reach a hand through, but perhaps with some kind of tool...</s></desc>

<desc><s>The bottom half of this set has a counter with a sink, a stovetop, a phone, various plastic utensils.</s> <s>Underneath is is a small drawer, a refrigerator, an oven, and two cubbies containing bins for the plastic props.</s> <s>The top half is purple, with a drying rack for plates, a window, a microwave, and two cabinets.</s> <s>You find <il></il> in it.</s></desc>

<desc><s>It has many cubbies, each taken up with something different.</s> <s>Most of the bottom shelves have bins containing toys, small race cars, LEGO bricks, etc.</s> <s>Some of the shelves are taken up by stuffed animals, old and worn out children's books, and other miscellaneous items.</s> <s>Nothing too exciting, aside from <il></il>.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's a short, blue basketball hoop with a green stand and green rim.</s> <s>It's made for children, so it would be very easy to just drop a ball into the hoop.</s></desc>

<desc><s>It's a short, plastic yellow desk for a child to sit at.</s> <s>A variety of COLORING BOOKS are spread out on its surface.</s> <s>You also find <il></il> on it.</s> <s>The desk has a few drawers, mostly containing crayons, colored pencils, and other art utensils.</s> <if cond="this.childPuzzle.solved === false"><s>The top drawer has an unusual knob that's shaped like some sort of hook.</s> <s>Opening it up, you can see it's screwed in where the usual knob would go.</s> <s>The screw seems to be a proprietary one that requires three prongs, so a normal screwdriver wouldn't work.</s></if><if cond="this.childPuzzle.solved === true"><s>The top drawer is missing a knob.</s> <s>Thankfully, you can stick a finger through the hole to open it, but there's nothing much of interest inside anyway.</s></if></desc>
<desc><s>They're splayed out on top of the desk.</s> <s>They all look pretty old, and every single drawing has already been colored in.</s></desc>

<desc><s>It's bright yellow, and made of a sturdy plastic.</s> <s>It has a keyhole on the front.</s> <if cond="this.childPuzzle.solved === true"><s>It's unlocked.</s> <var v="this.childPuzzle.alreadySolvedDescription.parseFor(player)" /></if><if cond="this.childPuzzle.solved === false"><s>It's locked, and there doesn't seem to be any way of opening it without the key.</s></if></desc>

<desc><s>It's an elaborate toy race track on top of a normal table.</s> <s>It has what looks like train tracks on it.</s> <s>It's electronically powered, meaning the cars can move autonomously.</s> <s>There are two loops, which make for an exciting race!</s> <s>You find <il></il> on it.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's a large, green bean bag chair.</s> <s>It looks pretty comfortable.</s> <s>Maybe you could sit down here and take a short rest.</s></desc>

<desc><s>It's a shallow blue ball pit with a little slide leading into it.</s> <s>As you might expect, it's filled with colorful plastic balls.</s> <s>You also find <il></il> in it.</s> <s>If you're really desperate, you might be able to hide in here.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's a black, plastic dome on a white, circular shell attached to the ceiling.</s> <s>It gives you the feeling that you're being watched.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's a small, flat-screen television with a beige, plastic border.</s></desc>
<desc><s>The floor is made up of the same EVA foam as in the main room.</s> <s>You find <il></il> on it.</s></desc>
<desc><s>The floor in this room is made up of polished white tiles.</s> <s>You find <il><item>a PILLOW</item> and <item>a COMFORTER</item></il> placed haphazardly on it.</s></desc>

<desc><s>It's a small, electronic switch just above the door handle.</s> <s>One end of it is labeled with a lock symbol, and the other end is labeled with an unlock symbol.</s> <s>This must be how you lock or unlock the door.</s> <s>It's currently <if cond="this.childPuzzle.solved">un</if>locked.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's a comfortable rolling chair.</s> <s>Nothing unusual about it.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's a black, plastic dome on a white, circular shell attached to the ceiling.</s> <s>It gives you the feeling that you're being watched.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's a small, flat-screen television with a beige, plastic border.</s></desc>

<desc><s>There's a table built into this surveillance station, as well as controls to switch the audio feed and play back old recordings.</s> <s>Unfortunately, the ability to play back old recordings is locked behind a password that you don't know.</s> <s>Figures.</s> <s>There's a monitor on the wall for almost every room in this facility: HALL 0, DORM 1, DORM 2, DORM 3, DORM 4, RESTROOM, HALL 1, HALL 2, HALL 3, HALL 4, HALL 5, GREENHOUSE, REC ROOM, PANTRY, BAR, KITCHEN, INFIRMARY, STORAGE, EXERCISE ROOM, CERAMICS STUDIO, PLAYROOM, and SURVEILLANCE.</s></desc>

<desc><if cond="findRoom('Hall 0').hasTag('video surveilled') === true"><s>The monitor is on, showing you a live surveillance feed from hall 0.</s> <if cond="findRoom('Hall 0').occupantsString !== ''"><s>You see <var v="findRoom('Hall 0').occupantsString" /> on the screen.</s></if><if cond="findRoom('Hall 0').occupantsString === ''"><s>You don't see anyone on the screen.</s></if></if><if cond="findRoom('Hall 0').hasTag('video surveilled') === false"><s>The monitor is on, but it's just showing you a blank screen.</s> <s>It seems the video feed must be disabled.</s></if></desc>

<desc><s>It's a standard red ping pong paddle.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's a plastic dart box with a clear window on the top of the case, allowing you to see what's inside.</s> <s>It opens up easily.</s> <s>In it, you find <il></il>.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's a small dart with a soft aluminum tip.</s> <s>You definitely couldn't do any damage with this.</s></desc>

<desc><s>It’s the classic board game Clue!</s> <s>Your objective as you play is to uncover who the killer is and how they committed the murder.</s> <s>You assume the role of one of six suspects and move around the board to collect clues.</s> <s>If you’re the killer, make sure to hide your tracks!</s></desc>

<desc><s>Originally created to decry capitalism, Monopoly has instead become representative of it.</s> <s>Oh well!</s> <s>Your objective is to move around the board obtaining various properties with the fake Monopoly money you’ve been given.</s> <s>You compete with other players, and sometimes strike deals with them, until you’re the last person left who hasn’t gone bankrupt.</s></desc>

<desc><s>This is a board game meant to mimic the reality of a person going through life by attending (or not attending) college, getting married, having kids, paying off mortgages, etc.</s> <s>It’s more fun than it sounds, really.</s> <s>You spin the spinner in the middle to determine how many spaces ahead you move, and the space you land on tells you what happens to your little person in-game.</s></desc>

<desc><s>It’s a game you should never play with an architect.</s> <s>Your objective is to pull pieces out from the neatly-stacked Jenga block tower without toppling the tower.</s> <s>Whoever topples the tower is out of the game!</s></desc>

<desc><s>It’s a playmat you place down on a floor, and then you spin a wheel to determine which spaces on the playmat you have to touch with your hands or feet.</s> <s>In the process, you’ll likely be getting tangled up with other players, which totally isn’t awkward or uncomfortable at all.</s></desc>

<desc><s>In this game, you each play with a variety of ships on your end, with a wall erected in between each player so you can’t see the other player’s pieces.</s> <s>You call out various coordinates, and if you guess correctly, the other player’s ship is sunk, until there are no more pieces left.</s></desc>

<desc><s>A game that has spawned many family feuds over what qualifies as a word, Scrabble is a game where you are given a jumbled-up variety of different letters, and you attempt to make words out of them on the board in front of you.</s> <s>Each letter and block is worth a certain amount of points.</s> <s>The winner is whoever has the most points at the end!</s></desc>

<desc><s>It's Mastermind, a code-breaking game for two players.</s> <s>In this game, one player is the codemaker, who creates a pattern of four pegs chosen from six different colors, and the other player is the codebreaker, who attempts to figure out the code in a limited number of turns.</s> <s>After each guess, the codemaker places smaller pegs that provide feedback to the codebreaker.</s> <s>It's an intense puzzle game that's sure to make you feel like an absolute genius or a total idiot!</s></desc>
<desc><s>You examine the pool stick.</s> <s>It's made of a hard metal.</s> <s>You could probably do some damage with it if you struck hard enough.</s></desc>
<desc><s>You examine the triangle.</s> <s>It looks like the kind you use to set up balls in a game of pool.</s></desc>
<desc><s>You examine the pool chalk.</s> <s>It's the kind that you use to rub the edge of a pool stick.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's a white ball used to hit the other balls in a game of pool.</s> <s>It feels pretty solid.</s></desc>

It's a silver cocktail shaker that has a strainer built into the top. You can use this for any cocktail that calls for it.

(Note: To make mixed drinks, `.drop` this onto the MIXING STATION, as well as the bottle or jug containing the ingredients you want, and a glass to pour the mixed drink into. Putting filled glasses will not work, unless the ingredient is only obtainable by one of the nozzles on the bar counter. When everything is in place, send `.use MIXING STATION`. If you're not sure what you can mix, hold this in your inventory and send `.recipes COCKTAIL SHAKER`!)

It's a small handheld whiteboard. If you found a marker, you could use this to write on. There's some writing already on it in black marker:
```ACETONITRILE (CH₃CN): A colorless liquid that converts to cyanide when inhaled, ingested, or absorbed through the skin. This process is inefficient due to the slow rate at which the body metabolizes it into hydrogen cyanide (HCN), so a high dosage must be ingested to ensure death by cyanide poisoning, which can take up to 2 hours. See the following instructions:
1. Fill beaker with distilled water.
2. Pour contents of beaker into 1000-mL flask.
3. Pour acetamide (CH₃CONH₂) into a weigh boat.
4. Transfer contents of weigh boat into flask.
5. Pour phosphorus pentoxide (P₄O₁₀) into a weigh boat.
6. Transfer contents of weigh boat into flask.
7. Swirl solution in flask until both chemicals fully dissolved.
8. Attach support ring to a ring stand.
9. Place flask with solution on support ring.
10. Position ring stand next to Bunsen burner such that the flask with solution is held above the flame.
11. Heat flask gently for 5 minutes.
12. Transfer contents of flask to another container and serve to unsuspecting victim.```

They're both lumpy and misshapen, with wildly uneven circumferences. The cup's handle is thin and flimsy, and feels like it might break off. Worse yet, the bottom of the cup is crooked, which makes it impossible for it to sit level on a flat surface; not that it matters, since the saucer has a rugged, bumpy surface itself. You'd be sure to spill whatever you put in this cup.They're of poor quality. The bottom of the saucer is decently sturdy, but it's not perfectly flat, preventing it from sitting completely level. It has a fairly flat surface, but the bottom of the cup is also a bit uneven, making it rock back and forth slightly. The saucer has a mostly even circumference at least, but the same cannot be said of the cup, which is lumpy all around. The handle is attached alright, but it's a little small, making it somewhat difficult to hold. You could probably drink out of this, but you'd have to be careful, or you'd make a mess.The craftsmanship is fairly decent. The cup and saucer both have sturdy bottoms that sit perfectly level on a flat surface. The saucer has a nice, even circumference with a concave rim. The sides and lip of the teacup are *mostly* even, but there are a few small divots and bumps here and there. The handle has a good thickness, with just enough room for your fingers. You could probably drink out of this just fine.The craftsmanship is *excellent*. The cup and saucer both have sturdy bottoms that sit level on any surface. The rim of the saucer is perfectly circular, and flares out to give it a concave shape, but its surface still has a completely flat indentation, in which the cup fits snugly. The body of the cup itself has perfect radial symmetry, and a very smooth texture. The handle is attached seamlessly, with the ideal shape to be held with just one finger. The cup's rim flares out to meet your lips, making it easy to sip from. They've already been fired in a kiln, but they've been coated with glaze. The glaze is clearredorangebrownyellowgreenteallight blueindigovioletpinkwhitegrayblack in color.

They're still wet, so you shouldn't drink out of the cup just yet. They should be fired in a kiln one more time before they're truly complete. Although, if you wanted to, you could choose another glaze and decide on a pattern to decorate them with.

This is a photo framecuptraydishboxlantern made of redwhite clay. It has thinthick edges, and a squarea rectangulara triangulara circularan ellipticala heart-shapeda diamond-shapeda star-shaped outlinebase. It was made by hand. It's of *terrible* quality. The edges are extremely rugged and misshapen, to the point where it barely resembles the shape it's supposed to be in. The bottom is wildly uneven, making it incapable of sitting level on a flat surface. Also, the lid is shaped and sized all wrong, so it doesn't securely cover the top.It's of poor quality. It has a fairly sturdy bottom, but it's not perfectly flat, preventing it from sitting level on a flat surface. The edges have many divots and bumps, giving it an irregular shape. It should be able to hold things, but you're not so sure about this one. The lid kind of fits in the indentation on top, but it rocks back and forth, and the knob is difficult to grip.The craftsmanship is fairly decent. It has a flat, sturdy bottom that sits perfectly level. The edges are mostly even, but it has a bit of a rough texture, with a few small divots and bumps here and there. The lid fits in the indentation on top quite well, but the knob is a little shallow, making it somewhat difficult to grip.The craftsmanship is *excellent*. It has a flat, sturdy bottom that sits level on any surface. The edges are perfectly symmetrical, and have a very smooth texture. The lid fits securely in the indentation on top, and has a sturdy, round knob that's easy to grip.

The clay is still wet. It needs time to dry before it can be put in the kiln. Although, if you wanted to, you could take some more time to carve a pattern into it.

It's a hi-tech tablet device with the number 1 on the back. Its screen is borderless and foldable, so you can fit it into a small space. The lock screen tells you the current time: ``.

You're able to unlock it without inputting any kind of password. It seems this device has all the standard functions you would expect in a tablet: a camera, a flashlight, Bluetooth connectivity, a note-taking app, a calculator app, and more. It's even connected to a Wi-Fi network; although there's no Internet browser, so it seems to be of limited use.

> Tip: This is an item. You can take it with the `.take` command, and then you can carry it around with you.

> Tip: Want to keep carrying this while also freeing up a hand? Try using the `.stash` command!

There's an app icon on the home screen labeled "Map". When you press it, the screen displays a map.

<desc><s>This is an incredibly soft pillow in a white pillowcase.</s> <s>You'd easily get a good night's sleep with this.</s></desc>

<desc><s>It's a small wire caddy to hold items you would use in the shower.</s><if cond="this.container !== null && this.container !== undefined && this.container.name.startsWith('SHOWER')"> <s>It's hung from the shower head, and pressed against the wall with suction force.</s></if> <s>In it, you find <il></il>.</s></desc>
<desc><s>The towel is pure white and perfectly clean.</s> <s>It smells fresh, and is incredibly soft to the touch.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's a small bar of lemon-scented soap.</s></desc>
<desc><s>It's a small bottle of lemon-scented shampoo.</s></desc>

<desc><s>It's a standard <procedural name="toothbrush color"><poss name="white">white</poss></procedural> toothbrush.</s> <s>None of the fancy bells and whistles you might get with an electric toothbrush can be found here.</s> <s>There are bristles, though.</s></desc>

<desc><s>It's a standard <procedural name="toothbrush color"><poss name="orange">orange</poss></procedural> toothbrush.</s> <s>None of the fancy bells and whistles you might get with an electric toothbrush can be found here.</s> <s>There are bristles, though.</s></desc>
